title: Lessingianthus correntinus
taxonomy: Lessingianthus correntinus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Lessingianthus, which consists of various species of flowering plants. The genus Lessingianthus is further classified under the family Asteraceae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants such as daisies and sunflowers. Lessingianthus correntinus is native to Brazil and is commonly found in the country's subtropical and tropical regions. The classification of Lessingianthus correntinus is a result of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.

intro: Lessingianthus correntinus, also known as Argentine chamomile or Argentine daisy, is a flowering plant native to South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating digestive issues, anxiety, and sleep disorders. The bioactive constituents of Lessingianthus correntinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
